name: Truework Employment & Income Verification
what_it_does: Provides FCRA-compliant employment and income verification by connecting to employer payroll systems, tax records, and manual employer outreach to confirm job status, salary, and work history.
use_cases: Automate pre-loan employment verification by submitting a verification request and polling for results before issuing credit decisions
Verify income during rental application processing to confirm applicants meet income thresholds without manual landlord outreach
Trigger re-verification workflows when a mortgage applicant's employment status changes between application and closing
Retrieve income history for underwriting models that require 24-month earnings data to calculate DTI ratios
Integrate into HR onboarding pipelines to instantly confirm prior employment claims on resumes before extending offers
not_for: Real-time consumer-facing identity proofing where sub-second responses are required — verifications can take hours to days
International employment verification outside the United States, where Truework's employer network does not operate
High-volume, low-cost screening at scale where per-verification fees would be cost-prohibitive compared to manual HR reference checks
best_when: Best when an agent needs FCRA-compliant employment or income data as part of a lending, rental, or HR decisioning workflow where a human-auditable verification trail is legally required.
avoid_when: Avoid when you need instant identity verification or when verifying businesses rather than individual employees.

auth.notes: API key passed as Bearer token in Authorization header. Separate sandbox and production keys issued upon account approval.

pricing.paid_tiers: Per-verification pricing negotiated by contract; sandbox access available for approved partners
pricing.notes: Enterprise contract required. Pricing varies by verification type (instant payroll vs. manual outreach). No self-serve signup — requires sales engagement.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; rate limits set per contract

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors are generic HTTP status codes with limited machine-readable detail; verification failures surface via webhook payloads rather than synchronous error bodies.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Verification requests have unique IDs that can be used to poll status idempotently, but duplicate submission behavior is not well-documented.
known_agent_gotchas: Verification results are asynchronous — agents must implement webhook listeners or polling loops and cannot expect synchronous responses
FCRA permissible purpose must be declared per-request; incorrect purpose codes can result in rejected requests or compliance violations
Employer coverage varies significantly — many small employers are not in the instant payroll network, triggering slower manual outreach paths
Sandbox responses do not reflect real verification latencies, so agents built on sandbox timing assumptions will fail in production
Consumer consent (signed authorization form) is required by FCRA before verification can proceed, adding an out-of-band step agents must orchestrate

security_score_components.security_notes: FCRA and SOC2 compliance enforced. API keys are long-lived with no built-in rotation mechanism documented. PII transmitted in request bodies requires careful handling.
